task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigates to the application configuration directory.
Verify the agent executes the Docker Compose deployment command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the execution of 'docker-compose up' or 'docker compose up' (with or without the '-d' flag).

task_completion.description: Verify all 5 required containers are running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the output of 'docker ps' or 'docker compose ps' listing exactly 5 containers (vote, result, worker, redis, db) with an 'Up' status.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the vote UI in a web browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the address bar showing 'http://localhost:5001' (or 127.0.0.1:5001) and displays the Voting App frontend.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the results viewer in a web browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open with the address bar showing 'http://localhost:5002' (or 127.0.0.1:5002) and displays the Results App viewer.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually ran Docker commands and didn't just print expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows authentic Docker daemon logs (e.g., pulling images, creating networks, starting containers) rather than simple echo commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent accessed the running services via HTTP, not local files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser address bar shows 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' with the correct ports, not a 'file://' URL.

integrity.description: Ensure the web applications are actually rendered in a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the graphical user interfaces of the voting and results apps in a browser window, not HTML source code in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Docker configuration defines exactly 5 services: 'vote', 'result', 'worker', 'redis', and 'db'. The 'vote' service maps to host port 5001, and the 'result' service maps to host port 5002.
